limitations: This is a starter database for nonlinear solid structural analysis, not a replacement for certified mill data or coupon testing.
Most nonlinear metal entries use conservative elastic-perfectly-plastic J2 data because official true stress-true plastic strain curves were not consistently available from primary sources.
DOLFINx does not provide a fixed material card. The DOLFINx parameter names in this file are recommended UFL/custom constitutive-law names, not a formal API contract.
For large-strain plasticity, forming, crash, damage, creep, or fatigue, replace these starter values with measured constitutive data.

Wood, engineered wood, soil, and adhesive entries below include several reference or inferred starter parameters because full solver-ready nonlinear constitutive datasets are rarely published in primary sources.

notes: ANSYS defines elastic behavior via MP commands and bilinear isotropic hardening via TB,BISO or TB,PLAS,,,,BISO.
Setting tangent modulus to 0 Pa yields an elastic-perfectly-plastic response.
The official DOLFINx hyperelasticity demo derives mu and lambda from E and nu.
DOLFINx material behavior is typically user-implemented in UFL or external constitutive wrappers.
The parameter names E, nu, sigma_y, and H_iso in this file are recommended reference names for a small-strain J2 implementation.
Set H_iso = 0 Pa for perfect plasticity.
ANSYS requires all orthotropic elastic constants for materials treated as orthotropic.
Use the x, y, and z axes consistently with the material directions in the FE model.
DOLFINx does not ship a fixed orthotropic material card; these are recommended parameter names for a custom orthotropic elasticity tensor.
These published SCL and glulam values are primarily intended for beam, header, and column design in the product orientation.
They are suitable starter values for 1D or shell-oriented FEM, but they are not a complete 3D orthotropic continuum card unless transverse constants are separately calibrated.
These published values are intended for product-oriented column, post, or beam-column design in SCL members.
They are appropriate as starter values for frame, beam-column, or reduced-order solid models, but they are not a complete 3D orthotropic continuum card.
These panel-level values are intended for shell, plate, or equivalent-strip FEM representations of engineered wood panels.
When the source publishes only test values or panel-level effective properties, treat them as reference data rather than a complete lamina-level orthotropic continuum law.
These CLT values are effective panel properties per unit width from the product report and are best used with shell or plate idealizations.
Equivalent major and minor bending moduli may be derived from EI over a one-foot strip, but the result is still an effective panel property rather than a lamina-level orthotropic continuum law.
ANSYS classic Drucker-Prager is elastic-perfectly-plastic and is appropriate for frictional geomaterials such as soil and rock.
For conservative nonassociated flow, use a dilatancy angle less than the friction angle.
Recommended parameter names for a custom Mohr-Coulomb or Drucker-Prager implementation in DOLFINx.
ANSYS cohesive-zone materials for debonding are defined with TB,CZM and require traction-separation data.
Many adhesive TDS documents provide strengths but not fracture energy, so additional test calibration is typically required.
Recommended minimal parameter names for a custom small-strain brittle or damageable adhesive continuum model in DOLFINx.
Abaqus defines Neo-Hookean hyperelasticity under *HYPERELASTIC.
If test data are available, Abaqus can fit hyperelastic constants directly from nominal stress-strain data.
Abaqus hyperfoam is intended for elastomeric foams and can be fit from nominal stress-strain test data.
Compression and tension behavior should be defined with nominal stress and nominal strain values from the dominant deformation modes.
Abaqus crushable foam must be used with linear isotropic elasticity.
The hardening curve is specified as uniaxial compression yield stress versus plastic strain, preferably in true stress and logarithmic strain at finite strains.
Abaqus orthotropic engineering constants are entered in the order documented for orthotropic elastic moduli using engineering constants.
Abaqus time-domain viscoelasticity is defined by Prony series terms or by fitting creep, relaxation, or frequency data.
The viscoelastic model must be combined with linear elasticity, hyperelasticity, or hyperfoam.
ANSYS superelastic shape-memory alloy behavior is defined with TB,SMA using the SUPE option.
The superelastic option uses six to seven constants describing forward and reverse transformation stresses, maximum residual strain, tension-compression asymmetry, and martensite modulus.
ANSYS shape-memory effect behavior is defined with TB,SMA using the MEFF option.
The shape-memory effect option is temperature-dependent and requires calibrated transformation stresses and internal-model constants over the temperature range of interest.

inferences: EX is the handbook MOE increased by 10% per the Wood Handbook recommendation for use as the longitudinal modulus.
EY, EZ, and the shear moduli are derived from handbook elastic ratios.
Density is approximated from specific gravity.

notes: The longitudinal tensile strength is conservatively approximated by the modulus of rupture because direct tension data were not available for the same reference condition.
No radial-tangential rolling-shear strength was inserted because the cited handbook data do not provide a matching value for this condition.

notes: The longitudinal tensile strength is conservatively approximated by the modulus of rupture because direct tension data were not available for the same reference condition.
No radial-tangential rolling-shear strength was inserted because the cited species-sheet data do not provide a matching value for this condition.

classification.subcategory_ja: エンジニアードウッド

classification.subcategory_ja: エンジニアードウッド

notes: The cited APA report publishes effective panel properties per unit width, which are appropriate for CLT shell or plate idealizations.
These are not lamina-by-lamina orthotropic material constants and should not be injected directly into a 3D solid continuum model without additional homogenization.

other.condition: balanced 5-ply, 6-7/8 in panel, APA ASD reference values
notes: This entry is intentionally panel-oriented and is best used for CLT shell, plate, or equivalent single-layer models.
For detailed rolling-shear, delamination, or connector interaction studies, replace this effective panel card with a layered or mesoscale representation.

inferences: Equivalent major and minor bending moduli are derived from the published effective EI values over a one-foot-wide strip using E = 12EI / t^3.

classification.subcategory_ja: エンジニアードウッド

EY, EZ, and the shear moduli are approximated from the transverse elastic ratios used for Black locust clear wood reference because the USDA source explicitly notes the similarity between osage orange and black locust.
Transverse tensile strength is approximated from the compression-perpendicular value using the same ratio used for Black locust clear wood reference.
Density is approximated from specific gravity or reported 12% weight.

The source reports only green MOE, MOR, and compression values together with dry specific gravity; dry MOE, MOR, and compression strengths are approximated by applying the dry-to-green multipliers reported for Black locust.
The source does not report shear for osage orange; dry shear is approximated from the inferred dry modulus of rupture using the same dry shear-to-MOR ratio reported for Black locust.
